content.full_text:
EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1028 Suite 522
mento 95814
22-5860

June 27, 1975

Mike Cullen, Member
California Legislature
State Capitol
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-137

Dear Mr. Cullen:

Thank you for your letter of June 12, 1975, requesting
an opinion with respect to guest privileges extended by a
golf club to elected officials.

Since your request raises no substantial questions
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act, your
request for a formal opinion is denied.

The provision of guest privileges to elected officials
does not make the golf club a lobbyist or a contributor to
a political campaign. The receipt of such privileges will
ordinarily be reportable by the elected officials under the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Although for the reasons stated no formal opinion
will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful.
If you have any further questions, I urge you to contact a
member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DHl:jp

MIKE CULLEN

[Address block - partially illegible]
443 WEST OCEAN BOULEVARD
LONG BEACH, CA 90802
MAIL: [illegible]

Admin [illegible] Auditor
TELEPHONE: [illegible]

Member
California Legislature

CHAIRMAN
CALIFORNIA INFORMATION SYSTEMS
IMPLEMENTATION COMMITTEE

ASSEMBLY COMMITTEES ON
Commerce, Drug & Co[?]
Transportation, [illegible]
Bonking and Community
Development

Request No. ________
Date Received ________
Response Due ________

June 12, 1975

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1015 L Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Dan:

For years a private golf club in Long Beach
has given guest privileges to elected officials
such as the late County Supervisor Burton
Chace, the mayor, and the appointed city
manager, among others. Since the passage of
Proposition 9, they are concerned whether the
giving of such privileges to honorary members
would classify the club as a lobbyist or a
major contributor to one's campaign. A regular
member pays about $1000 a year dues.

I have been asked to solicit an opinion from
the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Cordially,

MIKE CULLEN

LONG BEACH • CARSON • NAVAL BASE, LOS ANGELES-LONG BEACH
